title: [Rootless] Systemd fails to start pod generated service with 'PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.'
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

Systemd fails to start the service created on a rootless podman with the following error in `journalctl`. I've tried this on a Fedora 36 with podman 4.1.1 and 4.2.0-rc1 with the same results. 

```
Jul 21 13:46:49 fedora systemd[887]: Starting pod-log.service - Podman pod-log.service...
Jul 21 13:46:49 fedora systemd[887]: pod-log.service: New main PID 2989 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
Jul 21 13:46:49 fedora systemd[887]: pod-log.service: New main PID 2989 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
``` 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. As rootless user run the command:

```bash
$>[flotta@fedora ~]$ podman play kube - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: log
spec:
  containers:
    - name: log
      image: docker.io/eloycoto/logexample
EOF
```

2. Generate the systemd files inside user systemd directory
```bash
[flotta@fedora ~]$ cd ~/.config/systemd/user
[flotta@fedora ~]$ podman generate systemd --files --name log
```
3. Enable the two services created
```bash
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl enable --user pod-log
Created symlink /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/pod-log.service → /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/pod-log.service.
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl enable --user container-log-log.service 
Created symlink /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/container-log-log.service → /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/container-log-log.service.
```
4. start the service `pod-log`
```shell
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl --user start pod-log
Job for pod-log.service failed because the service did not take the steps required by its unit configuration.
See "systemctl --user status pod-log.service" and "journalctl --user -xeu pod-log.service" for details.
```

**Describe the results you received:**
The service starts but systemd complains that the `PID is not owned by root. Refusing` and eventually stops the service. The pod runs ok until systemd stops it:

```bash
Jul 21 14:03:27 fedora systemd[826]: pod-log.service: New main PID 2002 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
Jul 21 14:03:27 fedora systemd[826]: pod-log.service: New main PID 2002 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
Jul 21 14:03:27 fedora podman[2174]: 2022-07-21 14:03:27.923669377 -0400 EDT m=+0.049754830 container cleanup 958d0fdb90b075cafe5cd2ab65090a26b95a3f72c0d0eb5a7f598f4b55abca3d (image=localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-rc1-1657573142, name=c8ab6e7b5630-infra, health_status=, io.buildah.version=1.27.0-dev)
Jul 21 14:03:27 fedora podman[2165]: 2022-07-21 14:03:27.9640211 -0400 EDT m=+0.160844292 container stop 958d0fdb90b075cafe5cd2ab65090a26b95a3f72c0d0eb5a7f598f4b55abca3d (image=localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-rc1-1657573142, name=c8ab6e7b5630-infra, health_status=, io.buildah.version=1.27.0-dev)
Jul 21 14:03:27 fedora podman[2165]: 2022-07-21 14:03:27.992830607 -0400 EDT m=+0.189653997 container died 958d0fdb90b075cafe5cd2ab65090a26b95a3f72c0d0eb5a7f598f4b55abca3d (image=localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-rc1-1657573142, name=c8ab6e7b5630-infra, health_status=)
Jul 21 14:03:28 fedora podman[2165]: 2022-07-21 14:03:28.035178774 -0400 EDT m=+0.232001971 container cleanup 958d0fdb90b075cafe5cd2ab65090a26b95a3f72c0d0eb5a7f598f4b55abca3d (image=localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-rc1-1657573142, name=c8ab6e7b5630-infra, health_status=, io.buildah.version=1.27.0-dev)
Jul 21 14:03:28 fedora podman[2165]: c8ab6e7b5630-infra
Jul 21 14:03:28 fedora systemd[826]: pod-log.service: Failed with result 'protocol'.
Jul 21 14:03:28 fedora systemd[826]: Failed to start pod-log.service - Podman pod-log.service.
Jul 21 14:03:28 fedora systemd[826]: Dependency failed for container-log-log.service - Podman container-log-log.service.
Jul 21 14:03:28 fedora systemd[826]: container-log-log.service: Job container-log-log.service/start failed with result 'dependency'.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Service starts successfully and pod continues to run.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I've tested removing the `containers.conf` from /usr/share/containers/ with no different outcome. 

If I restart the service after it has been stopped it runs successfully:
```
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl status --user pod-log.service
○ pod-log.service - Podman pod-log.service
     Loaded: loaded (/var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/pod-log.service; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
     Active: inactive (dead) since Thu 2022-07-21 14:03:39 EDT; 5min ago
       Docs: man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
    Process: 2185 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start c8ab6e7b5630-infra (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
    Process: 2267 ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 c8ab6e7b5630-infra (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
    Process: 2276 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 c8ab6e7b5630-infra (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
   Main PID: 2200 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
        CPU: 280ms

Jul 21 14:03:28 fedora systemd[826]: Started pod-log.service - Podman pod-log.service.

Jul 21 14:03:39 fedora podman[2276]: 2022-07-21 14:03:39.134829269 -0400 EDT m=+0.041201069 container cleanup 958d0fdb90b075cafe5cd2ab65090a26b95a3f72c0d0eb5>

     Loaded: loaded (/var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/pod-log.service; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)

   Main PID: 2314 (conmon)
      Tasks: 3 (limit: 2331)
     Memory: 1.1M
        CPU: 96ms
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/pod-log.service
             ├─2311 /usr/bin/slirp4netns --disable-host-loopback --mtu=65520 --enable-sandbox --enable-seccomp --enable-ipv6 -c -e 3 -r 4 --netns-type=path />
             └─2314 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 958d0fdb90b075cafe5cd2ab65090a26b95a3f72c0d0eb5a7f598f4b55abca3d -u 958d0fdb90b075cafe5cd2ab65090a26b9>

```

When I check the status of the service with `systemctl --user status pod-log.service` I get this:
```
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl status --user pod-log.service

```
[flotta@fedora user]$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.2.0~rc1-2.fc36.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running a fresh install of a Fedora 36 Server on a VM, no other pods or containers running at the same time. The issue seems only to happen when starting the service for the first time.
The user has the XDG_RUNTIME_DIR and DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS set
```
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1000/bus
```

comments.body:
I found a workaround to set the flag `start` to false to create the containers but not start them. This avoids the PID issue since there is no PID to complain about when starting the service. 
```
[flotta@fedora ~]$  podman play kube --start=false - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: log
spec:
  containers:
    - name: log
      image: docker.io/eloycoto/logexample
EOF
```

The containers are there but in `Created` status:

```
[flotta@fedora ~]$ podman ps
c2e80ab1c59f  localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-rc1-1657573142              10 seconds ago  Created                                 39b89fe8a22d-infra
04f3963239a4  docker.io/eloycoto/logexample:latest                     4 seconds ago   Created                                 log-log
```
Create the systemd services
```
[flotta@fedora ~]$ cd ~/.config/systemd/user/
[flotta@fedora user]$ podman generate systemd --files --name log
```

Enable the services
```
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl --user enable pod-log.service
Created symlink /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/pod-log.service → /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/pod-log.service.
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl --user enable container-log-log.service 
Created symlink /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/container-log-log.service → /var/home/flotta/.config/systemd/user/container-log-log.service.
```
And finally start the service
```
[flotta@fedora user]$ systemctl --user start pod-log.service
```
The journalctl logs don't show the errors anymore:
```
Jul 21 18:37:34 fedora systemd[839]: Starting pod-log.service - Podman pod-log.service...
Jul 21 18:37:34 fedora systemd[839]: Started libpod-c2e80ab1c59f1880b0ccd4ed8c59203c9ab8db428121c48969e94ff816234de7.scope - libcrun container.

Jul 21 18:37:34 fedora podman[2088]: 2022-07-21 18:37:34.263087574 -0400 EDT m=+0.101376574 container start c2e80ab1c59f1880b0ccd4ed8c59203c9ab8db428121c48969e94ff816234de7 (image=localhost/podman-pause:4.2.0-rc1-1657573142, name=39b89fe8a22d-infra, health_status=, io.buildah.version=1.27.0-dev)

Jul 21 18:37:34 fedora systemd[839]: Starting container-log-log.service - Podman container-log-log.service...
